what_to_implement.module_signature: score_sectors(daily_panel: pd.DataFrame, sector_mapping: dict, as_of: date, lookback_days: int = 60) -> dict[sector, dict]
what_to_implement.returns: Per-sector dict with {return_60d: float, breadth_pct_up_60d: float, volume_60d_trend: float, composite_z_score: float, rank: int, members: list[ts_code]}

sector_metrics.return_60d: Equal-weighted mean return of sector members from as_of - 60 trading days to as_of
sector_metrics.breadth_pct_up_60d: Fraction of sector members with 60d return > 0
sector_metrics.volume_60d_trend: (mean(volume[-20:]) - mean(volume[-60:-20])) / mean(volume[-60:-20]) — recent volume vs older window

what_to_implement.composite: Equal-weighted z-score across the 3 metrics, then rank sectors. Top-K (default K=5) → 'trendy' sectors.
what_to_implement.output_format: JSON dict {sector_name: {...metrics, rank, members, is_top_k: bool}}
what_to_implement.PIT_discipline: as_of slices the panel to trade_date <= as_of. NO forward look.
what_to_implement.min_members_per_sector: Sectors with <10 active members are EXCLUDED from ranking (too small to be reliable)
what_to_implement.out_function: select_universe(top_k_sectors, members_alive_at_as_of) -> filtered list of tickers in trendy sectors

selftest_requirements: Synthetic: 50 tickers across 5 sectors, where Sector-A has all uptrending stocks and Sector-E has all downtrending → A should rank top, E bottom.
PIT: changing future data does not change rankings at as_of.
out_of_scope: Sentiment / news scoring (separate module, may not happen this week)
